articles.title: Lumentum’s fiery stock rally earns it a spot in this hot index - MarketWatch
articles.description: Lumentum’s stock performance ranks sixth in the S&P 500 this year. Now the optical company is set to join the Nasdaq-100.

articles.title: Gavin Newsom rejects gas tax suspension, blames Trump for soaring prices - New York Post
articles.description: California drivers are paying the highest gas prices in America, and Gov. Gavin Newsom still refuses to suspend the state gas tax as critics warn Sacramento’s energy policies are pushing the crisis…

articles.title: Trump reportedly plans to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary - Ars Technica
articles.description: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

articles.title: White House’s ‘lack of organization’ has AI lobbyists fretting - Politico
articles.description: The Trump administration has sent mixed signals about how tough it intends to be in vetting powerful new computer models.

articles.title: Whoop trackers will provide users with on-demand video access to licensed clinicians - Mashable
articles.description: The fitness tracker has new AI features coming too.

articles.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Score Record Closes On Iran Hopes; Nvidia Hits Highs (Live Coverage) - Investor's Business Daily
articles.description: Stock Market Today: The Dow Jones index rises Friday but lags the Nasdaq's jump. Chip stocks including Qualcomm spring higher.

articles.title: Momentum Trade at New Extremes Is Drawing Wall Street Warnings - Bloomberg
articles.description: The momentum trade — buy what’s winning, sell what isn’t — was already running red-hot heading into this week. Now a pause in the Iran war, strong US jobs data and another sharp rally in AI chips just piled on more fuel.

articles.description: Anthropic now relies on  a huge SpaceX data center for AI compute, tying Claude's rapid growth and reliability to Elon Musk.

articles.title: Memory Stocks Continue to Surge. What’s Driving Micron and Sandisk Higher. - Barron's
articles.description: Micron Technology and Sandisk stock continued their historic rally this week, thanks to a flurry of AI headlines signaling overwhelming demand and limited supply for memory.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high - TechCrunch
articles.description: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

articles.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul - TechCrunch
articles.description: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to…

articles.title: Tesla’s Latest Recall? Wheels May Fall Off Cybertrucks - WIRED
articles.description: In what is the 11th Cybertruck recall, certain models of Elon Musk’s embattled pickup could experience a sudden, unexpected wheel separation, thanks to the wrong grease and loose nuts.

articles.title: Jane Street earned $10bn in first quarter as it doubled trading revenue - Financial Times
articles.description: Results cement prop firm as one of the most profitable on Wall Street

articles.title: Oregon data centers must pay more for electricity, state regulators rule - OregonLive.com
Anthropic Inks $1.8 Billion Computing Deal With Akamai (AKAM) - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Anthropic PBC has signed a $1.8 billion computing deal with cloud services provider Akamai Technologies Inc. to meet surging demand for its artificial intelligence software, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: OpenAI makes its Mythos rival more widely available to cyber defenders - Axios
articles.description: Recent security testing suggests that GPT-5.5. model is nearly as good at finding and exploiting software bugs as Anthropic's Mythos Preview.

articles.title: PG&E failed to act on substation damage before crippling December blackout - The San Francisco Standard
articles.description: An independent probe found that high humidity levels inside the Mission substation sparked an outage that left 120,000 customers without power.
